meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to implement analytics with Antigravity and Claude Code to track progress and finish your first real-world AI app.
content.answer_block: Project abandonment stems from a lack of visibility into progress. By integrating lightweight analytics early, you transform abstract coding sessions into measurable data points. This creates a dopamine feedback loop, proving your app is alive and functional, which provides the psychological momentum necessary to push past the finish line.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: the initial spark of a brilliant idea, the feverish first night of coding, and then—the silence. The project sits half-finished, a graveyard of 'in-progress' repositories that haunt your GitHub profile. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a feedback loop.

In the professional world, we don't build in the dark. We use telemetry to see if our code is actually working for users. By installing analytics on day one, you turn your project into a living entity. This shift from 'coding' to 'product management' is the secret weapon that separates students who dream from engineers who ship.

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Select a lightweight provider like PostHog or Umami. The goal is to capture session data without bloating your codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular analytics service wrapper in TypeScript that initializes a PostHog client, ensuring it only tracks events in production environments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-dependency posthog-js && claude run 'initialize analytics service'

steps.title: Define Core User Milestones
steps.description: Identify the three critical actions that define success in your app. Tracking these keeps you focused on the user journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a tracking schema for user sign-up, core feature usage, and error triggers. Map these to a centralized event-tracking function.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'implement event tracking in key app components'

steps.title: Automate Error Telemetry
steps.description: Don't guess why your app is broken. Automatically log errors to your dashboard so you can fix them before they kill your motivation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an error boundary that captures stack traces and user metadata, then pushes them to the analytics dashboard as custom events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup global error boundary with analytics reporting'

steps.title: Build a Progress Dashboard
steps.description: Visualize your data. Seeing even a single 'Sign Up' event from a test user provides the validation needed to keep building.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard view in the analytics console that displays active users and feature completion rates for my project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude prompt 'create a summary report of top user events'

steps.title: Enable Continuous Feedback Loops
steps.description: Set up alerts for when specific events trigger. This forces you to engage with the app daily, keeping the project top-of-mind.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a webhook that sends a Slack or email notification whenever a user completes the primary project workflow.

pro_tips: Always track 'time-to-first-value'—if users take too long to get the point of your app, you will lose them and your motivation.
Use feature flags to decouple deployment from release; it allows you to test code in production without breaking the UX.
Ignore vanity metrics like 'page views'. Focus strictly on 'conversion events' that prove your core functionality works.
Treat your analytics dashboard like your project's heartbeat. If the graph is flat, you aren't building; you're just typing.
Automate the deployment of your analytics config using Claude Code to ensure consistency across every project you start.
content.next_steps: Now that your analytics are live, deploy your MVP to a free hosting service like Vercel or Railway and share the link with three friends to generate your first real-world data points.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the framework to finish what you start. Stop stalling and start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
